transit_systems.name: BART
transit_systems.description: Bay Area Rapid Transit — heavy rail serving SF and East Bay
transit_systems.frequency: Every 15–20 min most hours, 15 min peak

stations.name: Embarcadero
stations.notes: Busiest SF station, Ferry Building, Financial District access

stations.name: Montgomery Street
stations.notes: Financial District core, many office workers

stations.name: Powell Street
stations.notes: Union Square, cable car turnaround, heavy tourist traffic

stations.name: Civic Center
stations.notes: City Hall, UN Plaza, Tenderloin edge

stations.name: 16th Street Mission
stations.notes: Mission core, very active platform, near Valencia corridor

stations.name: 24th Street Mission
stations.notes: Southern Mission, family neighborhood, good taquerias nearby

stations.name: Glen Park
stations.notes: Village stop, canyon park, quieter end of Mission district transit

stations.name: Balboa Park
stations.notes: Multi-modal hub — BART, Muni, bus, Excelsior edge

stations.name: Daly City
stations.notes: First stop outside SF, large parking hub, East Bay transfer

transit_systems.name: Muni Metro
transit_systems.description: SF Municipal Railway light rail — underground Market St trunk, surface through neighborhoods
transit_systems.fare: $3.00 per boarding, free transfers for 90 min

stations.name: N-Judah at Ocean Beach
stations.notes: Terminal at Great Highway

stations.name: N-Judah at 9th/Irving
stations.notes: Inner Sunset hub

stations.name: N-Judah at Duboce
stations.notes: Transfers to J-Church, surface tunnel portal

stations.name: Caltrain/4th & King
stations.notes: Transit hub — Caltrain terminus, Muni subway

stations.name: J-Church at 30th/Church
stations.notes: Noe Valley surface section

stations.name: J-Church at Castro
stations.notes: Castro station underground

stations.name: T-Third at Bayview
stations.notes: Third Street surface — Bayview community hub

stations.name: T-Third at 4th/King
stations.notes: Shared with N terminus

stations.name: L-Taraval at West Portal
stations.notes: West Portal underground-surface transition

stations.name: L-Taraval at Ocean Beach
stations.notes: Taraval/Great Highway terminal

stations.name: West Portal Station
stations.notes: Key transfer hub for multiple Muni Metro lines

stations.name: Castro Station
stations.notes: Underground Castro station, all surface lines converge

stations.name: Church Station
stations.notes: Market & Church, surface-underground transition point

stations.name: Van Ness Station
stations.notes: Market & Van Ness underground

stations.name: Civic Center Station
stations.notes: Shared with BART Civic Center

lines.N: Judah — Inner Sunset to Caltrain via Market St tunnel
lines.J: Church — Noe Valley to Embarcadero via Castro
lines.M: Ocean View — Balboa Park to Embarcadero via West Portal
lines.T: Third — Bayview waterfront to Caltrain/4th & King
lines.S: Castro Shuttle — Embarcadero to Castro via Market

curated_neighborhoods.name: The Mission
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Vibrant Latino heart of SF — murals, taquerias, Mission Dolores, gentrification tension, bike lanes, nightlife on Valencia

curated_neighborhoods.name: Castro
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: LGBTQ+ cultural center, Victorians, rainbow crosswalks, Harvey Milk legacy, arts, boutiques, Twin Peaks backdrop

curated_neighborhoods.name: Noe Valley
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Quiet, affluent, stroller-dense, brunch culture, 24th Street commercial strip, valley warmth sheltered from fog

curated_neighborhoods.name: Potrero Hill
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Sunny hill with views, industrial past giving way to tech, good microclimate, dog walkers, converted warehouses

curated_neighborhoods.name: Bernal Heights
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Tight-knit hilltop community, dog park at the summit with panoramic views, artsy, family-oriented, good burritos

curated_neighborhoods.name: Glen Park
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Quiet village feel, canyon park, BART stop, more residential and calm than its neighbors

curated_neighborhoods.name: SoMa
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: South of Market — tech offices, SFMOMA, Moscone Center, leather bars, homeless encampments, nightclubs, live-work lofts

curated_neighborhoods.name: Tenderloin
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Dense, difficult, resilient — immigrant restaurants, social services, SROs, Little Saigon, city's hardest edge

curated_neighborhoods.name: Civic Center
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Government buildings, UN Plaza, Davies Symphony Hall, City Hall dome, homeless services, government workers

curated_neighborhoods.name: Hayes Valley
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Boutiques, wine bars, Patricia's Green, opera-crowd cafes, gentrified after freeway removal, design-conscious

curated_neighborhoods.name: North Beach
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Beat generation legacy, Italian restaurants, Vesuvio bar, City Lights bookstore, Coit Tower, tourists and old-timers

curated_neighborhoods.name: Chinatown
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Dragon gates, herbalists, dim sum, crowded streets, one of the oldest in the US, Grant Avenue tourism vs Stockton St locals

curated_neighborhoods.name: Russian Hill
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Lombard Street, steep streets, city views, quiet residential, cable car routes, literary history

curated_neighborhoods.name: Nob Hill
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Old money, grand hotels (Fairmont, Mark Hopkins), cable cars, Grace Cathedral, city views, very steep

curated_neighborhoods.name: The Tenderloin
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: See Tenderloin

curated_neighborhoods.name: Fisherman's Wharf
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Tourist epicenter — sea lions at Pier 39, crab stands, Alcatraz ferries, Ghirardelli Square, foghorns

curated_neighborhoods.name: Marina
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Young professionals, brunches, Chestnut Street boutiques, Palace of Fine Arts, bay views, earthquake-prone fill

curated_neighborhoods.name: Cow Hollow
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Upscale, Union Street shops and restaurants, between Marina and Pacific Heights, affluent but walkable

curated_neighborhoods.name: Pacific Heights
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Mansions, consulates, city views, very expensive, Lafayette Park, 'Billionaires' Row' on Broadway

curated_neighborhoods.name: Japantown
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Japan Center mall, Peace Pagoda, ramen shops, sake bars, cultural programming, smaller than it once was

curated_neighborhoods.name: Western Addition
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Historically Black neighborhood, Fillmore jazz legacy, painted ladies, mosaic of cultures after urban renewal

curated_neighborhoods.name: Fillmore
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Fillmore Street strip, jazz history, music venues, sushi row, transitional between Japantown and Pacific Heights

curated_neighborhoods.name: Haight-Ashbury
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Summer of Love epicenter, head shops, Victorians, hippie legacy meets current vintage stores, close to GG Park

curated_neighborhoods.name: Lower Haight
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Grittier sibling of Haight, bars and small venues, working class, tattooed locals, less touristy

curated_neighborhoods.name: Alamo Square
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: The Painted Ladies, postcard panorama, park with dog walkers and tourists, between Western Addition and Hayes Valley

curated_neighborhoods.name: Inner Sunset
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Irving Street cafes, foggy mornings, close to GG Park and UCSF, diverse families, N-Judah streetcar

curated_neighborhoods.name: Outer Sunset
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Foggy beach town within a city, surfers at Ocean Beach, Dutch windmills, Vietnamese restaurants, quiet

curated_neighborhoods.name: Inner Richmond
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Clement Street — Asian restaurants, European bakeries, bookshops, fog belt, working class families

curated_neighborhoods.name: Outer Richmond
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Quiet fog, Balboa Street, Lincoln Park, Baker Beach access, more residential and local than Inner Richmond

curated_neighborhoods.name: Sunset District
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Broad umbrella for Outer/Inner Sunset — fog capital of SF, largely residential, N-Judah along Judah St

curated_neighborhoods.name: Excelsior
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Working-class Latino neighborhood, Mission Street extension, affordable, McLaren Park, diverse immigrants

curated_neighborhoods.name: Dogpatch
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Industrial waterfront being gentrified — craft breweries, design studios, historic buildings, T-Third streetcar

curated_neighborhoods.name: Bayview
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Southeast SF, historically Black, Hunters Point naval shipyard redevelopment, community gardens, food deserts

curated_neighborhoods.name: Visitacion Valley
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Southern tip, working class, diverse Asian and Latino residents, McLaren Park, quiet residential

curated_neighborhoods.name: Portola
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Between Excelsior and Bernal Heights, diverse, Mission Street commercial strip, relatively unknown to outsiders

curated_neighborhoods.name: Embarcadero
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Ferry Building, Ferry Plaza Farmers Market, Bay Bridge views, the waterfront promenade, Financial District edge

curated_neighborhoods.name: Financial District
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Suits, skyscrapers, lunch hustle, BART stations, transamerica pyramid, quiet on weekends

curated_neighborhoods.name: Union Square
curated_neighborhoods.vibe: Retail heart — Westfield mall, hotels, theaters, cable car turnaround, Powell BART, very tourist-dense

curated_landmarks.name: Golden Gate Park
curated_landmarks.description: 1,017-acre urban park — de Young museum, Japanese Tea Garden, Conservatory of Flowers, bison paddock, dutch windmills, Polo Field

curated_landmarks.name: Dolores Park
curated_landmarks.description: Mission's social hub — picnics, views of downtown, Castro on one side, J-Church streetcar

curated_landmarks.name: Alamo Square Park
curated_landmarks.description: The Painted Ladies panorama, steep hill, Victorian row houses, popular with tourists and dog walkers

curated_landmarks.name: Buena Vista Park
curated_landmarks.description: Forested hilltop park, oldest park in SF, overgrown trails, panoramic views, cruising history

curated_landmarks.name: McLaren Park
curated_landmarks.description: Second-largest park in SF, overlooked by tourists, community gathering, hiking, disc golf

curated_landmarks.name: Glen Canyon Park
curated_landmarks.description: Wild canyon in the middle of the city, creek, native plants, neighborhood dogs

curated_landmarks.name: Bernal Heights Park
curated_landmarks.description: Summit park with 360° views, dogs off-leash all over the hill, radio tower, beloved by locals

curated_landmarks.name: Lafayette Park
curated_landmarks.description: Sunken city block park, Pacific Heights living rooms overflow here, dogs, tennis courts

curated_landmarks.name: Duboce Park
curated_landmarks.description: Popular dog park, N-Judah streetcar passes alongside, Duboce Triangle neighborhood

curated_landmarks.name: Ocean Beach
curated_landmarks.description: Wild Pacific shore — strong undertow, surfers in wetsuits, bonfires at night, foggy and windswept

curated_landmarks.name: Baker Beach
curated_landmarks.description: Clothing-optional beach with Golden Gate Bridge backdrop, Marin Headlands view

curated_landmarks.name: Aquatic Park
curated_landmarks.description: Protected cove, cold-water swimmers, bocce ball, Maritime Museum, cable car terminus nearby

curated_landmarks.name: Crissy Field
curated_landmarks.description: Restored tidal marsh and beach along the bay, Golden Gate views, cyclists, dog walkers, kite fliers

curated_landmarks.name: Embarcadero Waterfront
curated_landmarks.description: Ferry Building to Bay Bridge — promenade, farmers market, bay views, tourist ferries

curated_landmarks.name: Ferry Building
curated_landmarks.description: 1898 Beaux-Arts terminal, Saturday farmers market, artisan food vendors, bay views

curated_landmarks.name: City Hall
curated_landmarks.description: Gilded dome (taller than DC's Capitol), Harvey Milk was shot here, civic events

curated_landmarks.name: Coit Tower
curated_landmarks.description: WPA murals inside, Telegraph Hill views, feral parrots, steps from North Beach

curated_landmarks.name: Twin Peaks
curated_landmarks.description: Two 922-foot summits, panoramic 360° city view, tourist overlook and local hiking

curated_landmarks.name: Lands End
curated_landmarks.description: Rugged coastal bluffs, labyrinth, shipwreck views, Sutro Baths ruins, GGNRA trail

curated_landmarks.name: Sutro Baths
curated_landmarks.description: 1896 glass-enclosed baths ruins, now a tidal pool at low tide, atmospheric

curated_landmarks.name: Palace of Fine Arts
curated_landmarks.description: Roman rotunda and colonnade reflected in a lagoon, swans, wedding photos, the Marina's jewel

curated_landmarks.name: de Young Museum
curated_landmarks.description: Fine arts, American art, textiles, tower with free views over GG Park, Golden Gate Bridge

curated_landmarks.name: California Academy of Sciences
curated_landmarks.description: Natural history — living roof, aquarium, planetarium, rainforest dome, all under one roof in GG Park

curated_landmarks.name: SFMOMA
curated_landmarks.description: Modern art anchor of SoMa, expanded Snøhetta wing, free to walk through ground floor

curated_landmarks.name: Fillmore Auditorium
curated_landmarks.description: Legendary music venue, posters are part of the mythology, shaped SF rock history

curated_landmarks.name: Haight & Ashbury Street
curated_landmarks.description: The intersection — street sign tourists, head shops, Summer of Love memorial ground zero

curated_landmarks.name: Alcatraz Island
curated_landmarks.description: Former federal penitentiary, ferry from Pier 33, bay views, seabirds

curated_landmarks.name: Fort Mason
curated_landmarks.description: WWII port turned cultural center — SFMOMA rental, farmer markets, theater, Bay views

curated_landmarks.name: Precita Eyes Murals
curated_landmarks.description: Mission mural tradition radiating from Precita Park, community art tours

curated_landmarks.name: Tartine Manufactory
curated_landmarks.description: The bread line that defines the Mission's food culture — sourdough, pastries, coffee

street_corridors.name: Valencia Street
street_corridors.vibe: The Mission's gentrification spine — restaurants, bookshops, bars, bike lane, taquerias fading to artisanal

street_corridors.name: Mission Street
street_corridors.vibe: Working-class corridor, pan-Asian restaurants, dollar stores, Muni 14, the real Mission

street_corridors.name: 24th Street
street_corridors.vibe: Southern Mission heart — tortillerias, carnicerias, indie cafes, community murals

street_corridors.name: Market Street
street_corridors.vibe: SF's main artery — Muni Metro, Castro to Ferry Building, bike lanes, Pride parade route

street_corridors.name: Castro Street
street_corridors.vibe: The Castro's vertical strip — bars, bookshops, Castro Theatre marquee, rainbow crosswalks

street_corridors.name: Haight Street
street_corridors.vibe: Head shops to vintage stores — tourist Haight blending into grittier Lower Haight bars

street_corridors.name: Fillmore Street
street_corridors.vibe: Jazz history, sushi row, boutiques — ties Pacific Heights to the Fillmore's legacy

street_corridors.name: Divisadero Street
street_corridors.vibe: Gentrifying corridor, brunch spots, barbershops, pan-Asian, NoPa heart

street_corridors.name: Chestnut Street
street_corridors.vibe: Marina's boutique strip — fitness, brunch, weekend shopping, young professionals

street_corridors.name: Union Street
street_corridors.vibe: Cow Hollow's upscale row — wine bars, antiques, restaurants, Victorians

street_corridors.name: Columbus Avenue
street_corridors.vibe: North Beach's diagonal — Italian restaurants, City Lights, Vesuvio, leads to Chinatown edge

street_corridors.name: Grant Avenue
street_corridors.vibe: Chinatown's tourist face — shops, dim sum, dragon gates — shifts to North Beach above Broadway

street_corridors.name: Clement Street
street_corridors.vibe: Inner Richmond's main street — Asian restaurants, Russian bakeries, produce shops, very local

street_corridors.name: Irving Street
street_corridors.vibe: Sunset's café row — N-Judah runs alongside, bookstores, tea shops, close to UCSF

street_corridors.name: Judah Street
street_corridors.vibe: N-Judah runs down the middle to Ocean Beach — surf shops, family restaurants, foghorns

street_corridors.name: Third Street
street_corridors.vibe: T-Third streetcar corridor — industrial becoming creative, Dogpatch craft breweries to Bayview

street_corridors.name: Embarcadero
street_corridors.vibe: Bay-facing boulevard — Ferry Building to AT&T Park, cyclists, joggers, bay air

street_corridors.name: The Great Highway
street_corridors.vibe: Ocean Beach edge road — closed to cars on weekends, surfers parking, foghorns, windswept

street_corridors.name: 19th Avenue
street_corridors.vibe: City spine through the avenues — mostly cars, connects GG Park to Daly City

street_corridors.name: Potrero Avenue
street_corridors.vibe: Hospital row (SF General), auto shops, transitional between SoMa and Potrero Hill

inter_city.notes: Scenic coastal route, Coast Range and Willamette Valley. Departs once daily.
Passes through Santa Barbara, San Luis Obispo, the Central Coast.
30+ daily flights. BART from downtown to SFO in ~30 min.
Through the Transbay Tube. ~15 min, very frequent.
Peninsula commuter rail, bullet trains ~1h15, locals longer.
Scenic bay crossing with Golden Gate views. Sausalito = bike-friendly.

weather_config.microclimate_note: SF has strong microclimates — Mission is warmer than Sunset by 10-15°F on most days
